question: HOW CAN I CHECK MY TIMING?
content:
Why video is the gold standard for timing
Timing in bowling is not a feeling, it’s a relationship between your body and the ball at a precise moment. Human perception is unreliable here because the approach happens too fast. Video removes opinion and emotion.
How to check timing accurately:
1.	Record from the side, perpendicular to the lane (not behind or angled).
2.	Scrub the video frame by frame.
3.	Pause the exact frame your slide foot becomes flat and stable at the foul line.
4.	Look at the ball position in that same frame.
This moment is non negotiable. It is the reference point for all timing evaluation.
What the Ball Position Means at That Moment
At the instant your slide foot is flat:
Ball near parallel to the floor
→ Timing is correct
Ball already dropping
→ Timing is early (the ball arrived before the body)
Ball still high in the backswing
→ Timing is late (the body arrived before the ball)
There is no gray area here. This snapshot tells the truth every time.
A Universal Timing Rule:
If your slide foot is flat and stable while the ball is still parallel to the floor, your timing is correct.
This rule is universal because it is based on physics, not style.
It applies to:
One handed bowlers
Two handed bowlers
Any number of steps
Any tempo (slow or fast)
Any approach style
Everything else in the approach,(pushaway, step count, rhythm, tempo…) is simply a method to arrive at this exact relationship at the foul line.
So why This Moment Matters More Than Anything Else
At the foul line, three things must arrive together:
1.	The body (slide foot stable)
2.	The ball (ready to fall, not already falling)
3.	Gravity (about to take over naturally)
When these line up, bowling becomes easy.
When they don’t, you compensate, and compensation creates problems.
When timing is correct, you don’t try to do these things, they happen on their own.
Balance improves:
The ball’s weight is synchronized with your body’s forward motion, so it supports you instead of pulling you off balance.
Revs increase without effort:
The ball falls into the release instead of being forced. Gravity creates leverage, not muscle.
Speed becomes stable:
Early timing causes dumping, late timing causes muscling. Correct timing produces consistent speed naturally.
The release cleans up:
You stop grabbing, steering, or spinning the ball because you’re no longer late or rushed.
Physical strain is reduced:
Bad timing forces the body to save the shot. Good timing lets the body stay relaxed.
Shots repeat under pressure:
Timing holds up when nerves hit. Muscling and compensation do not.
The Big Truth Most Bowlers Miss:
Bowlers often chase symptoms individually, but they all originate from the same broken relationship at the foul line.
You don’t fix timing by thinking about timing.
You fix timing by adjusting the approach so the ball and body arrive together at the foul line.
Once that relationship is correct:
The body organizes itself
The swing becomes free
The release simplifies
The game becomes repeatable
